title: Unknown variant on Serialize enums round-trips as literal string 'unknown'
description: Trigger: a struct containing ChatKind, DeliveryState, OwnerPresence, etc. is serialized after receiving an unknown value from the server. The Unknown catch-all variant serializes as the variant name lowercased ('unknown') via #[serde(rename_all = 'lowercase')], producing a value indistinguishable from a hypothetical spec-defined 'unknown' kind. The original wire value is permanently lost. In practice these types are deserialization-only, but the public Serialize impl makes this reachable. Mitigation: doc comment on each Unknown variant noting that serializing it produces the literal string 'unknown', not the original wire value.
close_reason: Added doc notes to all Unknown variants documenting serialization-as-literal-unknown behavior. Added test_unknown_catch_all_variants_serialize_as_literal_unknown documenting the serde limitation.
